name: Compose Log Aggregation
description: Aggregate, filter, and follow logs across multiple Docker Compose services in a single terminal view.

steps.title: Follow logs for all services
steps.description: Stream logs from every service in the Compose stack.

steps.title: Follow logs for specific services
steps.description: Focus on logs from one or more specific services.
steps.tip: List multiple service names to filter to just those services.

steps.title: Show logs with timestamps
steps.description: Add timestamps to log output for correlation.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Compose Log Aggregation
set -euo pipefail

SERVICE=${1:-""}

echo "=== Docker Compose Services ==="
docker compose ps --format 'table {{.Name}}\t{{.Status}}'

echo ""
if [ -n "$SERVICE" ]; then
  echo "=== Following logs for: $SERVICE ==="
  docker compose logs -f -t --tail=100 "$SERVICE"
else
  echo "=== Following logs for all services ==="
  docker compose logs -f -t --tail=50
fi

faq.question: How do I limit log output to avoid flooding the terminal?
faq.answer: Use --tail=N to show only the last N lines, and --since=1h to show logs from the last hour.

faq.question: Can I save Compose logs to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. Use 'docker compose logs > logs.txt' or configure a logging driver in your compose file (e.g., json-file with max-size).
